query:
=== Recent conversation (last 0 exchanges) ===
[Assistant] **Phase 1 findings — presenting before extraction:**

**Session**: 1 session, 573 records, 46m, 0% rework — clean run.

**What went well (positive outliers):**
- Permission friction: 0 vs mean 14.8
- Bash-for-search: 54 vs mean 155.6 (tool discipline excellent)
- No coordinator respawns
- Knowledge stored: 11 vs mean 8.7

**Hotspots:**
- **[WARNING] F-01** — 45 compile cycles in testing phase, peak 17 in 5min at +20m. Rust-dev was iterative on struct changes.
- **[WARNING] F-02** — `context_get` failed 6×, `Read` failed 4× in fix phase at session start.
- **[info] F-03** — Tester piped cargo output through multiple filters (output parsing struggle)
- **[info] F-04** — 13 files re-read (rust-dev navigating worktree)
- **[info] F-05** — 4 sleep workarounds in testing phase

**Stewardship** (agents stored 11 entries during cycle — known: #3696 lesson, #3698 pattern, #3701 lesson). Quality check + full extraction now.
[tool: mcp__unimatrix__context_search(bugfix-426 freshness half-life) → #1468 | Retrospective findings: bugfix-252 | lesson-learned | [feature_cycle:bugfix-252, hotspot_count:1, source:retrospective] | 0.48
#3252 | ExtractionRule must not perform side effects: maintenance actions belong in maintenance_tick, not in ProposedEntry output | lesson-learned | [background-tick]
=== End recent conversation ===

entries.title: Complete all struct/field definition changes before first compile in multi-type iterative fixes
Implementation checklist items from approved design must be enumerated in the rust-dev spawn prompt — not left implicit in the investigator report
How to edit a large Rust file (1000+ lines): read full context and identify all affected sites before making any changes
